mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 230 in the CPIAUCSL index signals central bank intervention risks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative when both INDPRO and GDPC1 show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate elevated demand pressures, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns during demand-driven expansion regimes.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 20d returns despite concurrent HOUST and RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains (PAYEMS) reflect a tightening labor market, pushing equity prices higher through increased consumer spending over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when PAYEMS exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 7% signals labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when UNRATE falls below 7.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and declining UNRATE indicates a robust labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS, lead to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns during periods when FEDFUNDS rises by >0.5% in 3 months.
